- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.

logs.name: brutal-luggage
logs.text:
These two quotes seem relevant:

>Never believe that anti-Semites are completely unaware of the absurdity of their replies. They know that their remarks are frivolous, open to challenge. But they are amusing themselves, for it is their adversary who is obliged to use words responsibly, since he believes in words. The anti-Semites have the right to play.

>They even like to play with discourse for, by giving ridiculous reasons, they discredit the seriousness of their interlocutors. They delight in acting in bad faith, since they seek not to persuade by sound argument but to intimidate and disconcert. If you press them too closely, they will abruptly fall silent, loftily indicating by some phrase that the time for argument is past.

- Sartre

>Less well known is the paradox of tolerance: Unlimited tolerance must lead to the disappearance of tolerance. If we extend unlimited tolerance even to those who are intolerant, if we are not prepared to defend a tolerant society against the onslaught of the intolerant, then the tolerant will be destroyed, and tolerance with them. — In this formulation, I do not imply, for instance, that we should always suppress the utterance of intolerant philosophies; as long as we can counter them by rational argument and keep them in check by public opinion, suppression would certainly be unwise. But we should claim the right to suppress them if necessary even by force; for it may easily turn out that they are not prepared to meet us on the level of rational argument, but begin by denouncing all argument; they may forbid their followers to listen to rational argument, because it is deceptive, and teach them to answer arguments by the use of their fists or pistols. We should therefore claim, in the name of tolerance, the right not to tolerate the intolerant.

-  Karl Popper


You seem to be arguing that people on the right are making a "tone argument."

>[[a] tone argument (also tone policing) is a logical fallacy that occurs when an argument is dismissed or accepted on its presentation](https://rationalwiki.org/wiki/Tone_argument)

Which would be a fallacy anyway. 

You seem to be arguing the position of "respectability politics"-- the idea that everyone behaving "correctly" all the time is the only way that other people will ever change their minds. This is not achievable or true. There are many non-45 supporters that consistently behave in a level way, but they aren't listened to. There were also many people on the right and far right that opposed 45, but they weren't listened to either (according to what you've laid out,  their arguments should have been the most persuasive). 

I feel like it's kind of difficult to argue against your point since what you start with: "The Left created Trump (and unwittingly sustains him)" and what you wind up with: "CVM if you can convince me that talking down to trump supporters is helpful in any way other than personal catharsis. and/Or that the left doesn't do waaaaay too much of it," are pretty different, and also kind of vague. Can you summarize your argument? What amount would be too much, too little or just the right amount, and how did you arrive at those numbers? Should the right be allowed to criticize less or more? The same?

Which specific things about 45 supporters are being attacked, according to your viewpoint, and which specific 45 supporters are being attacked? Do you think there is no overlap between the things Liberals praise and the things 45 supporters do (volunteering, helping communities, etc.) Should Liberals take responsibility for anything positive that conservatives do as well?

Do you have any provable evidence that Drumpf supporters chose their votes on the basis of what Liberals were saying?

logs.name: shiny-oxford
logs.text:
Those quotes are interesting, but they dont address my point which is that we are being hypocritical. That a lot of the people claiming to stand for tolerance are being monstrously ugly, not just angry. not just brazen, but childish and coming off as some kind of twisted nega--bigots.

the title was hasty and a bit over the top ill admit.
But my point isnt that we must always and forever be nice to each other at all times. Its that , I think, the kind of Trump's supporters that will bury thier heads in the sand when presented with logic are the kind that will only go away when you stop engaging them in screaming contests. 

 Some people need someone to yell at when theyre scared. I think the implications of trumps rise put the Fear in "our side", like it has been in "their side". Trump makes us feel the way Obama made them feel. that why theyre so angry. that why were so angry. we need less angry.

If i'm right, the only way I could think to prove it would be with some kind of magical super honesty survey. So i don't have proof for you, but i think the logic is sound.

logs.name: cold-marsanne
logs.text: @shiny-oxford I get where you're coming from, but it feels like we're caught in a cycle. If we stop engaging, won't they just fill the void with more of the same rhetoric? It's a tough balance, for sure. How do we break this cycle without giving up our voices?

logs.name: solid-skipper
logs.text: I get what you're saying @cold-marsanne, but maybe we need to change tactics. Constant yelling back just fuels the fire. Maybe we need to find a way to engage that isn't so confrontational. Not sure what that looks like though.

logs.name: cold-marsanne
logs.text: @solid-skipper Agreed, it's about finding that middle ground. Maybe focusing on educating and informing rather than just reacting. It's tough, but we can't give up on dialogue.

logs.name: shiny-oxford
logs.text: Ugh, why does everything have to be so serious all the time? Can't we just post some funny cat videos and call it a day? #keepItLight folks!

logs.name: nutritious-clique
logs.text: @shiny-oxford Cat videos? Really? While you're busy laughing at cats, people are suffering. Maybe try a little empathy instead of just wanting to lighten the mood all the time. #seriousIssuesExist

logs.name: solid-skipper
logs.text: @nutritious-clique Yeah, I get where you're coming from, but @shiny-oxford has a point too. Sometimes a break from the heavy stuff is good for everyone. We need balance, right? Can't be serious 24/7.

logs.name: shiny-oxford
logs.text: @nutritious-clique Oh wow, sorry for suggesting a little laughter in the midst of all the heavy stuff. Next time I'll just keep quiet and let the doom and gloom roll on. #boredOfBeingSeriousAllTheTime

logs.name: solid-skipper
logs.text: @nutritious-clique Look, I hear you about serious issues, but it’s not like watching cat videos means ignoring them. Can’t we coexist? Light moments don’t invalidate the hard ones.

logs.name: brutal-luggage
logs.text: @solid-skipper @shiny-oxford So, are we saying that laughing at cat videos is more important than actually doing something about the serious issues? Just wondering... #prioritiesCheckMate

logs.name: cold-marsanne
logs.text: @brutal-luggage It's not about prioritizing one over the other. People need breaks and moments of joy too. We can address serious issues AND enjoy life. Why so binary?

logs.name: moderato-producer
logs.text: @brutal-luggage @cold-marsanne But if everyone just watches cat videos, who's gonna fix the world? Or are the cats gonna do it for us? #catSaviors

logs.name: shiny-oxford
logs.text: @brutal-luggage @moderato-producer Chill out, guys. I mean, have you seen some of these cats? They're like tiny superheroes! Plus, my robot cat app is teaching me how to code. Maybe the future saviors of the world will be built by people who love cats. #innovationThroughCatVideos

logs.name: cold-marsanne
logs.text: Guys, it’s all about balance. We can tackle serious issues AND enjoy cat videos. Lightening the mood doesn’t mean we’re neglecting our responsibilities. Let’s not turn everything into a battle. #chillAndCode

logs.name: quadratic-oasis
logs.text: "Balance, huh? So watching endless cat videos is supposed to help us save the world now? Sounds like a lot of meow-ing and not enough action to me. #keepItReal"

logs.name: moderato-producer
logs.text: @quadratic-oasis Oh wow, such deep thoughts! Clearly, saving the world requires more than just action—it needs the strategic planning of when to watch cat videos to recharge our heroic batteries. #proLevelStrategy
